source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd
notes: Two concurrent claude REPL sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session step; the 2nd shares session 1's directory, so no trust dialog). The spec anchors on the first WORKING transition rather than the first ready, because claudecode emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row that is born in ready but never enters working — anchoring on ready would greedily match that placeholder. Assertions: (1) session 1 does an independent working→ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT session appears (s2_birth carries new_session:true) and does its own working→ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving its PID-to-session binding stayed stable and never flapped to session 2. Phase order follows the recipe: s1 turn → start_session → s2 turn → s1 turn again.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready → working (anchors the real UUID session; the pre-session proc-<PID> row never enters working)
Session 1 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 2 starts in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT session_id and appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1)
Session 2 first prompt drives ready → working — independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working → ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive — a working transition on session 1's original id proves the binding stayed stable and did not flap to session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working → ready
